title: How a 26-Year-Old Single Mom Built Her Tech Career While Raising Two Kids
guestBio: Hannah White is a 26-year-old IT professional at Tomahawk and single mother of two boys. Despite facing the challenges of single parenthood at a young age, she has built a successful career in technology while maintaining fierce determination and grit. She represents the next generation of women breaking barriers in tech while juggling the demands of motherhood.
excerpt: Hannah White proves that age and circumstances don't define your potential. At just 26, this single mom is crushing it in tech while raising two boys and discovering her own power along the way.
metaDescription: Single mom Hannah White shares how she built her tech career at 26 while raising two kids. Real talk on juggling motherhood, career growth, and finding your power.
keyTakeaways: Single motherhood doesn't disqualify you from building a successful career in competitive fields like technology.
Finding your power often happens through facing challenges head-on rather than avoiding them.
Technology tools can be game-changers for busy working mothers trying to streamline their lives.
Age is not a barrier to leadership and professional success when you combine grit with determination.
Building a support system and community is essential for thriving as a single working mother.
Your current circumstances are not your final destination if you're willing to hustle for your goals.
Owning your power means making decisions based on your values rather than others' expectations.

pullQuotes.text: Your current circumstances are not your final destination if you're willing to hustle for your goals.

timestamps.label: Finding Your Power at Any Age

content:
What does it look like to build a thriving tech career while raising two children as a single mother? Hannah White is living proof that it's not only possible but that you can absolutely crush it while doing so. At just 26 years old, Hannah has carved out her space in the technology world at Tomahawk while navigating the beautiful chaos of motherhood with fierce determination and an inspiring amount of grit.

In this raw and honest conversation, Hannah opens up about the realities of her journey, the tools that keep her sane, and how she's learned to own her power in ways that many people twice her age are still figuring out. This isn't a fairy tale story about having it all figured out. This is real talk about what it means to hustle hard, make tough choices, and refuse to let your circumstances define your ceiling.

Breaking Into Tech as a Young Mother

Hannah's path into technology wasn't a straight line, and honestly, the best careers rarely are. What sets her apart isn't that she avoided challenges but that she met them head-on with a mindset that refuses to accept limitations. The tech industry can be intimidating for anyone, but as a young single mother, Hannah faced additional layers of complexity that would have caused many people to choose what seemed like easier paths.

Instead, she chose to see her unique perspective as an asset rather than a liability. The problem-solving skills you develop as a mother, the ability to multitask under pressure, and the fierce protectiveness that drives you to create a better future for your children are exactly the qualities that make someone excel in technology roles. Hannah recognized this and leveraged it, proving that sometimes what looks like a disadvantage on paper becomes your greatest strength in practice.

The key insight here is that you don't have to wait until your life circumstances are perfect to pursue your professional goals. Perfect timing is a myth that keeps too many talented women on the sidelines. Hannah's story demonstrates that you can build something meaningful right where you are, with what you have, while managing the responsibilities that matter most to you.

The Daily Reality of Juggling Everything

Let's be honest about what juggling a demanding career and single motherhood actually looks like day to day. It's not about achieving perfect balance because perfect balance doesn't exist. It's about making intentional choices about where your energy goes and being strategic about the systems and tools you use to support your goals.

Hannah has become a master of efficiency out of necessity, and the strategies she's developed are gold for any working mother. She's learned to identify the technology solutions that actually save time versus the ones that just add more complexity to her life. This distinction is crucial because as busy mothers, we can't afford to waste precious time on tools that promise the world but deliver confusion.

The reality is that successful working mothers aren't superhuman. They're strategic. They know that saying yes to one thing means saying no to something else, and they've gotten comfortable making those trade-offs based on their values and long-term goals rather than trying to please everyone or do everything perfectly.

Technology as Your Secret Weapon

One of the most practical aspects of Hannah's approach is how she uses technology to streamline her life rather than complicate it. Too often, we get caught up in trying every new app or system that promises to solve our organizational challenges, but Hannah has learned to focus on solutions that actually integrate into her real life.

The tech tools that truly make a difference are the ones that eliminate steps rather than add them, that work seamlessly in the background rather than requiring constant attention, and that solve genuine pain points in your daily routine. Hannah shares specific examples of how she's automated certain aspects of her life to free up mental energy for the things that truly require her attention and creativity.

This approach extends beyond just personal productivity tools. In her professional role, Hannah has learned to identify and implement solutions that create real value, a skill that has made her incredibly valuable to her team. The ability to see through the noise and focus on what actually works is a superpower in both technology careers and motherhood.

Owning Your Power at Any Age

Perhaps the most inspiring aspect of Hannah's story is how she's learned to own her power at such a young age. Too often, we think that confidence and leadership are things that come with decades of experience, but Hannah proves that power comes from making decisions aligned with your values and being willing to advocate for yourself and what you need.

Owning your power doesn't mean having all the answers or never feeling uncertain. It means trusting yourself enough to make decisions based on your own judgment rather than constantly seeking permission or validation from others. It means being willing to take up space in rooms where you might be the youngest person or the only mother.

For Hannah, this has meant being direct about her needs as a working mother, setting boundaries that protect her time with her children, and refusing to apologize for the unique perspective she brings to her work. She's learned that when you own your story instead of trying to hide parts of it, you become more powerful, not less.

The permission Hannah gives herself to be ambitious while being a devoted mother is something every working woman needs to hear. Your dreams don't have to get smaller because your responsibilities got bigger. In fact, having people depending on you can be the exact motivation you need to think bigger and take bold action toward your goals.

Hannah White's story is a powerful reminder that your current chapter is not your final destination. Whether you're a single mother wondering if you can build the career you want, a young professional feeling like you need to wait your turn, or anyone who's been told their circumstances make success unlikely, Hannah's example shows that determination, strategic thinking, and the willingness to own your power can take you exactly where you want to go.
